sentences.base_language: My friend looks a bit sad today.
sentences.target_language: 私の友達は今日少し悲しそうです。
sentences.romanized: Watashi no tomodachi wa kyō sukoshi kanashisō desu.
sentences.literal: My friend today a-little sad-seeming is.
sentences.grammarNote: 〜そう (sō) = 'looks like / seems'. Attach to い-adjective stem: 悲しい → 悲しそう (looks sad), 嬉しそう (looks happy), 疲れそう (looks tired). This is the Japanese equivalent of 看起来 (kàn qǐlái) in Chinese — a sensory impression suffix.

sentences.target_language: 何かあったか聞きます。
sentences.grammarNote: Embedded questions in Japanese use the plain form + か: 何かあったか (whether something happened). Unlike English, no word-order inversion is needed. か inside a sentence doesn't end the sentence — it embeds the question.

sentences.base_language: He says he had an argument with his girlfriend.
sentences.target_language: 彼女と口喧嘩したと言っています。
sentences.romanized: Kanojo to kuchigenka shita to itte imasu.
sentences.grammarNote: 〜と言っている (to itte iru) = 'says that…'. The quotation particle と (to) links reported speech to the verb of saying/thinking: 〜と言う (say that), 〜と思う (think that). This と is different from the listing と (and).

それは大変でしたね (that must have been tough) is the natural Japanese empathetic response. It's more natural than a literal translation of 'I'm sorry to hear that'. The ね at the end invites shared feeling. 大変 (taihen) = 'tough / hard / terrible'.

sentences.base_language: I tell him it will be okay.
sentences.target_language: きっと大丈夫だよと伝えます。
sentences.romanized: Kitto daijōbu da yo to tsutaemasu.
sentences.grammarNote: 大丈夫 (daijōbu) = 'okay / alright / fine'. The よ at the end of a plain-form clause before と adds a tone of assertion or reassurance. 伝える (tsutaeru) = 'to convey / tell' (implies the message reaches the person, unlike just 言う).

sentences.target_language: 良い友達はお互いに支え合います。
sentences.romanized: Yoi tomodachi wa otagai ni sasae-aimasu.
sentences.grammarNote: お互いに (otagai ni) = 'mutually / each other', equivalent to Chinese 互相 (hùxiāng). 〜合う (au) attached to a verb stem creates a reciprocal meaning: 支え合う (support each other), 助け合う (help each other), 話し合う (discuss with each other).

sentences.base_language: He feels a little better after talking.
sentences.target_language: 話した後、少し気分がよくなりました。
sentences.romanized: Hanashita ato, sukoshi kibun ga yoku narimashita.
sentences.literal: Talk-did after, a-little mood good became.
sentences.grammarNote: 〜くなる (ku naru) = 'become more [adjective]': よくなる (get better), 悪くなる (get worse), 楽しくなる (become more fun). Past tense: よくなりました (has become better). The Chinese equivalent is 好一些了 with 了 for change of state.

sentences.target_language: それが友達というものですよ。
sentences.romanized: Sore ga tomodachi to iu mono desu yo.
